key_insight: INCARBench shows that LLMs can often produce plausible VASP INCAR settings, but scientifically valid configurations require much stricter task-critical correctness than semantic similarity alone.
engineering_relevance: For computational materials workflows, reliable AI assistants must respect coupled physics constraints such as DFT+U and magnetism, not just generate syntactically or semantically reasonable inputs.
method: Built INCARBench to evaluate LLMs on VASP INCAR configuration generation and repair
Tested 19 model configurations across semantic accuracy, policy accuracy, and task-critical correctness
Analyzed failures in coupled settings and compared repair ability with configuration preservation
evidence_signal: Across 19 model configurations, several models scored well on semantic/policy accuracy, but task-critical correctness was much lower, especially for coupled physics settings.
watch_item: High preservation of already-valid parameters remains difficult, so deployment should include validation checks before running expensive simulations.
paper_title: INCARBench: A Benchmark for Scientific Configuration in VASP INCAR by Large Language Models
